fetchStatus: Blocked by Vercel security checkpoint; content reconstructed from web search and Alchemy guide
summary: Arbitrum is an Ethereum L2 using optimistic rollups. EVM-compatible (existing Solidity contracts, tools, and wallets work without modification). Reduces costs ~10x compared to Ethereum L1 while maintaining similar security.

Arbitrum One mainnet: Chain ID 42161. Arbitrum Sepolia testnet: Chain ID 421614. Gas token: ETH. Sub-second confirmations (~0.3-0.5s blocks). Public RPCs available but no WebSocket support on public endpoints.

The quickstart tutorial builds a 'CupcakeVending Machine' Solidity contract deployed via Remix IDE. Workflow: write Solidity -> compile in Remix -> deploy to local Foundry node -> test -> deploy to Arbitrum Sepolia -> deploy to Arbitrum One.
Development tools: Remix IDE (browser-based), Hardhat, Foundry (forge/cast/anvil), Alchemy/QuickNode for RPC access. Smart contracts verifiable on Arbiscan (sepolia.arbiscan.io).
Arbitrum Nitro stack: ArbOS system-level executor + Arbitrum Virtual Machine. Same EVM instruction gas costs as Ethereum. Sepolia testnet mirrors the Nitro production stack.

workstreamTags.BUILD: Arbitrum is LEVER's Q3-Q4 2026 multi-chain expansion target. EVM-compatible means LEVER's Solidity contracts deploy without modification. Chain ID 42161 (mainnet), 421614 (Sepolia testnet). Same Foundry tooling works. Key difference from Base: Arbitrum has no Coinbase distribution advantage but has 30.86% L2 TVL share and deeper DeFi ecosystem (GMX, Camelot, etc.).
workstreamTags.ADVISOR: Arbitrum expansion is on the roadmap but not urgent. Base is the right launch chain (Coinbase ecosystem, 46.6% L2 TVL). Arbitrum adds ~31% more L2 TVL addressable market. Multi-chain should wait until Base traction is proven.
